tier_remaining_note: Tier A remains fully cleared (0). Tier B recomputed from a disk-reconciled rerun of generate_build_plan_v2.py's scoring model on 2026-07-21 (ROUTES_DIR fixed locally to the non-Windows path for this run only, the script file on disk still has the Windows-style path and needs a permanent fix): 333 remaining before chunk 92, minus the 50 Tier B pairs chunk 92 built, leaves 283. Not yet covered by verify_build_state.py's automatic disk reconciliation (only route_pairs/blog/total_markdown_files are); re-run the generator manually each chunk until that gap is closed.
notes: Chunk 92 DONE (Template B, Tier B, 50 new routes, 2 blocks, one batch). Built via 8 parallel research agents, one per cluster, taking the 50 highest-scored unbuilt Tier B pairs from a disk-reconciled rerun of generate_build_plan_v2.py's scoring model (6,871 built, Tier A=0, Tier B=333 remaining before this chunk). Chunk-91-completion cluster (8 routes): united-kingdom-to-sudan.md (Sudan's own import side freshly researched; Qatar Airways' new Doha-Port Sudan service, 3x/week from 2 July 2026, is a genuinely new gateway distinct from Khartoum) plus united-states-to-X for the 7 countries chunk 91 left one-direction-only (Antigua and Barbuda, Gabon, Grenada, Libya, Papua New Guinea, Saint Lucia, Sudan), reusing each country's already-verified export side and researching the US import side and Sudan's own import side fresh. Iceland reciprocal cluster (8 routes): both directions with Netherlands, New Zealand, Switzerland and China. Iceland confirmed on New Zealand's MPI recognised rabies-free list (same tier as Japan/Singapore/Hawaii) and on GACC's China rabies-free list, but absent from New Zealand's and China's own reverse-direction recognition, a genuine asymmetry; Iceland's mandatory 14-day MAST quarantine (any origin, confirmed still in force) applies regardless of Category 1 status. Macau-South Korea reciprocal cluster (2 routes, brand new pair): Korean Air and Jeju Air both fly Macau-Incheon nonstop, unusually direct for a Macau route, but Korean Air's A321neo on that route is excluded from carrying cargo-hold pets under its own policy, so only cabin-sized pets can use it. Canada cluster (32 routes, the bulk of this chunk): closed out Canada as the only missing destination/origin for 21 countries that already had full UK/US/UAE/Australia coverage (Albania, Algeria, Angola, Bahamas, Belarus, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Cameroon, Eswatini, Ivory Coast, Kosovo each both directions; Kyrgyzstan, Lesotho, Madagascar, Malawi, Moldova, Mongolia, Montenegro, Mozambique, North Macedonia, Senegal, Tajikistan, Turkmenistan outbound-from-Canada only, reverse legs deferred to a future chunk). CFIA's high-risk-for-dog-rabies list (verified live against inspection.canada.ca, dated 13 March 2025) was checked individually per country rather than assumed as a block: Albania, Bahamas, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Kosovo confirmed NOT high-risk; Algeria, Angola, Belarus, Cameroon, Eswatini, Ivory Coast confirmed high-risk. Critically, CFIA's own 28 September 2022 notice text was verified to ban only commercial dog imports from high-risk countries, explicitly leaving personal pets out (CFIA has only said it would 'explore options' to tighten personal-pet rules later, not that it has), a materially lighter system than the CDC's titre-test/six-facility-airport machinery used for the US and a genuine differentiator against several already-published Canada sibling pages that don't mention their own destination's high-risk status at all. Confirmed CFIA has no DAFF-style approved-country-group or residency-staging system at all, a real structural difference from Australia's 180-day rule that several of these countries' existing Australia pages describe. Belarus surfaced its own distinct airspace-ban timeline: Canada banned Belarusian aircraft in March 2022 (tied to the Russia invasion), a separate and earlier trigger from the EU/UK's May 2021 Ryanair-incident ban, same practical routing outcome. Montenegro confirmed to apply its titre test with no origin exemption for Canada either, consistent with the asymmetry flagged in earlier chunks. Canada's own Annex II Part 2 EU listing was verified to extend Moldova's and North Macedonia's titre exemptions to Canada on its own merits, not borrowed from the UK/EU relationship. One fabrication risk caught and corrected mid-draft: an agent initially wrote an unsourced 'Turkish Airlines refuses pets on UK-connected flights' claim onto a Canada page with no Canada-specific basis, rewritten to an honest 'unconfirmed, verify directly' framing before the file was finalised. QA pass after all 8 agents finished: zero em dashes, en dashes or curly quotes across all 50 files (verified with a Python Unicode scan); zero banned vocabulary (four watch-list substring hits manually confirmed harmless: 'fostering' inside CFIA's own commercial-dog definition, 'embarkation'/'disembarkation' aviation terms, and the 'PetEmbark' brand name, consistent with the same false-positive pattern logged in chunks 84, 87 and 89); correct Marcus Webb persona and template_variant B on all 50; every export_requirements/import_requirements block verified as a keyed YAML map via a Python parser; route_complexity restricted to the valid low/moderate/high/very_high tokens; zero corpus-wide title/description collisions checked against the full 6,921-file corpus; zero broken sideways/upward links, each verified against files confirmed to exist on disk before inclusion; no PetTransportGlobal contact email/phone found on any file. Routes 6,871 -> 6,921, pages 7,677 -> 7,727. Chunk 93 (Template C, Tier B) is next.
_previous_notes_chunk_91: Chunk 91 DONE (Template A, Tier B, 50 new routes, 2 blocks, one batch). Built via 7 parallel research agents, one per cluster, taking the 50 highest-scored unbuilt Tier B pairs from a disk-reconciled rerun of generate_build_plan_v2.py's scoring model (6,821 built, Tier A=0, Tier B=383 remaining before this chunk). UAE outbound cluster (17 routes), Germany outbound cluster (5 routes), Singapore reciprocal cluster (8 routes: corrected Singapore's AVS Category A/B/C/D naming to the current Schedule I/II/III system, confirmed live 21 July 2026), three brand-new-country clusters (20 routes: Antigua and Barbuda, Gabon, Grenada, Libya, Papua New Guinea, Saint Lucia, Sudan to/from UK+US, with UK-to-Sudan and US-reverse-legs deliberately left for chunk 92). CDC high-risk and UK Part 1/Part 2 status independently verified per country. Honest uncertainty flagged on Libya's and Sudan's export processes given their administrative situations. Routes 6,821 -> 6,871, pages 7,627 -> 7,677. Chunk 92 (Template B, Tier B) was next.
_previous_notes_chunk_90: Chunk 90 DONE (Template E, Tier B, 50 new routes, 2 blocks, one batch). Built via 8 parallel research agents, one per country/hub cluster. Australia/UAE reciprocal cluster (36 routes): closed the reverse legs for the 18 countries chunk 89 opened one-direction-only. Turkmenistan cluster (3 routes). New Macau hub cluster (6 routes, brand-new pairs): Hong Kong, Japan and South Africa both directions with Macau. UAE reverse-leg cluster (5 routes). Key finds: DAFF non-approved countries each picked a source-justified staging country; Montenegro confirmed DAFF Group 3 and MOCCAE low-risk, a genuine lighter outlier; MOCCAE's post-titre wait cross-checked against USDA APHIS's own UAE page (no fixed wait); flydubai/Etihad/Somon Air/Air Macau carrier exclusions found fresh. Two pre-existing sibling-page issues flagged, not fixed: several Australia-bound pages wrongly imply Group 3 skips the 180-day wait and that Perth is a valid PEQ landing point; Kosovo/Kyrgyzstan/Lesotho UK/US pages link to non-existent /pet-transport/origins/ pages. Routes 6,771 -> 6,821, pages 7,577 -> 7,627. Chunk 91 (Template A, Tier B) was next.

counts.note: total_site_pages counts content .md source files. The full deployed total (including Hugo taxonomy and list pages) is verified from sitemap.xml after a build.

counts_method: Reconciled from disk by verify_build_state.py. Never hand-edit count fields; run 'python verify_build_state.py --write' instead.
_dropped_from_rolling_window: Chunks 80, 81, 82-83, 84, 85-86, 87, 88 and 89 notes were dropped to cap file growth (git history still has them in full at commits 4ecac2f1, 3741f752, ef4d776e, ca50c52f, bd68e3c5, 0cac6bc3, 6bb8b070 and 0e8acb9b). A rolling window of the two most recent previous-chunk notes (currently chunk 91 and chunk 90) is kept going forward.
